svg.text: Generated from repository JSON results. Planned public benchmarks are not drawn as wins.
200 facts, 50 natural-language queries, same hash embeddings.
Hotness, TTL, correction, stale suppression, namespace isolation.
Synthetic long-history evidence retrieval: profile, preference, correction, TTL, namespace, and filler noise.
This is the small deterministic regression benchmark. Public LoCoMo and LongMemEval runs below carry the stronger external evidence.
WaveMind-only local runs on NumPy exact index. Quality stays high; dynamic ranking latency is the next target.
Current fact from JSON: static p@1 is 0.94 at 5000 memories; dynamic policy p@1 is 1.00 through 5000 memories.
Target: keep public retrieval quality at Chroma/Qdrant parity while cutting dynamic latency below 20 ms at 5000 memories.
Official public datasets with identical embeddings per run. These are retrieval/evidence checks, not final answer-quality scores.
Latency: NoMIRACL RU WaveMind 10.22 ms vs Chroma 2.60 ms; LongMemEval full WaveMind 7.27 ms vs Chroma 15.96 ms.
Generated 128-d vectors, 100 queries, recall@10 against exact cosine neighbors. Latest point: 50000 vectors.
Latency at 50000: NumPy 6.49 ms; int8 24.92 ms; Annoy 4.92 ms; Qdrant local 43.49 ms.
Completed public runs are evidence. Planned rows below are the next proof path, not claimed wins.
Also planned: MTEB Retrieval, full-corpus MIRACL Russian, LMEB, RAGBench, official VectorDBBench, and service-mode Qdrant/FAISS curves.
